// GOOD: domain model with clear ownership boundaries
struct User {
    name: String,
    order_ids: Vec<u64>,
}

struct Order {
    id: u64,
    user_id: u64,
    items: Vec<OrderItem>,
}

struct OrderItem {
    product_id: u64,
    quantity: u32,
}

struct Product {
    id: u64,
    name: String,
    price: f64,
}

struct Store {
    users: HashMap<u64, User>,
    orders: HashMap<u64, Order>,
    products: HashMap<u64, Product>,
}

impl Store {
    fn create_order(&mut self, user_id: u64, product_id: u64) -> u64 {
        let order_id = self.next_id();
        let item = OrderItem { product_id, quantity: 1 };
        let order = Order {
            id: order_id,
            user_id,
            items: vec![item],
        };
        self.orders.insert(order_id, order);
        self.users.get_mut(&user_id).unwrap().order_ids.push(order_id);
        order_id
    }
}
